text: Laparoscopy was started using five trochars.
Omentectomy was performed towards the left side.
This is a partial omentectomy which leaves about three to four centimeters of omentum from the gastroepiloic vessels.
The left gastroepiloic vessels were dissected and divided with a hybrid energy device.
Dissection of lymph node station 4SB was completed.
You can see the spleen on the right lower side.
On the lesser curve, you can see the area containing vessels from the left gastric artery, station number 3A, and the right gastric artery, station number 3B.
A perpendicular line was drawn from the inferior margin of station number 3A to the greater curvature.
Clearing the omentum of the lesser curve was performed.
At this point you have to be careful not to injure the branches from the right gastric artery.
Clearing of the greater curve was done along with ligation of the right gastroepiloic vessels.
The stomach was then resected by linear staplers with two gold load 60 millimeter cartridge.
You have to be careful not to injure the remaining stomach.
Putting a gauze between the caudate lobe and the inferior vena cava below helps to protect these structures during lymph node dissection.
Lymph node dissection around the celiac axis starting from the upper border of the pancreas was performed.
The right gastric artery has to be preserved in proximal gastrectomy, so dissection of station number 8A was done from the medial side of the right gastric artery.
The common hepatic artery was identified while dissecting lymph node station number 8A.
Dissection of station number 11P along the splenic artery behind the pancreas was performed.
Lymph node station number 7 dissection was done towards the left gastric artery.
The left gastric vein was identified in the process and divided.
Left gastric artery was skeletonized for ligation and division.
Dissection of lymph node station number one was performed.
Soft tissue around the esophagus was carefully resected including the anterior vagus nerve.
The posterior aspect of the esophagus including the posterior vagus nerve was cleared out.
Dissection of posterior stomach was done exposing the upper pole of the spleen.
Careful clearing of the remaining tissue around the esophagus was done for proximal resection.
A laparoscopic bulldog clamp was inserted for distal clamping of the esophagus.
A laparoscopic purse string clamp was inserted.
Each end of the double-sided straight needle suture was inserted through the two holes on the purse string clamp.
The suture was then taken outside of the abdomen.
The esophagus was divided with an electrocautery device.
Short gastric arteries were divided with a hybrid energy device.
The left lower port wound was extended four centimeters to the medial side.
It is used for specimen extraction inastomosis.
The specimen was extracted.
The proximal margin above the bulldog clamp was cut for frozen sections.
The specimen was opened along the greater curvature.
There was a subtle lesion between the two preoperatively applied clips.
A wound protector was used at the extended port side.
The operator and the assistant grabbed and pulled the full layer of the esophagus horizontally.
The assistant grabbed the end of the anvil to keep it from slipping out.
The purse string sutures were tied extracorporeally.
The trits ligament was identified by finding the inferior mesenteric vein in the mesocolon.
The jejunum was taken out for extracorporeal anastomosis.
The jejunum was transected, which makes an A-loop on the right and an E-loop on the left.
The esophagogastric anastomosis site was marked with a marking pen.
10 cm was calculated and marked as the length between the gastrojejunostomy and the esophagogastrostomy site.
6 cm was marked distal for the insertion of the stapler.
Then, 15 cm was measured for the jejuno-jejunostomy site.
Anastomosis between the A loop side of the proximal jejunum and the marked spot on the distal jejunum was done with a linear stapler with white load 60 mm cartridge.
The entry closure was done with absorbable barbed sutures.
The mesenteric defect was closed with non-absorbable barbed sutures.
The stump of the e-loop of proximal jejunum was held with two alice clamps and opened.
A 25 millimeter circular stapler was inserted through the opened jejunal stump until the marked spot.
A nylon tie was made to prevent slipping out.
The circular stapler was then inserted into the abdomen.
The circular stapler was opened by the assistant.
It was then inserted into the anvil and closed, completing the docking sequence.
The e-loop was checked to prevent overlap of stapling site.
After stapling was successful, the circular stapler was pulled out carefully.
The entry hole was closed by a linear stapler with the white load 60 millimeter cartridge.
The wound was opened for extracorporeal gastrojejunal stomy anastomosis.
The inferior tip of the distal stomach was opened and tagged with vicryl sutures.
The marked site on the jejunum was pulled out.
An opening was made at the antemisenteric side and also tagged with victoral sutures.
Linear stapler with blue load 60 millimeter cartridge was inserted into the stomach in the jejunum.
The gastric stump was pulled superiorly in order to make the anastomosis away from the existing stapler line.
The entry hole was closed with an absorbable barbed suture.
The gastrojejunal stomy was completed.
A few reinforcement sutures were made and tied extracorporally.
The Peterson's defect, which is located between the A loop and the mesocolon, was closed with non-absorbable barbed sutures.
Proximal gastrectomy with double track anastomosis was completed.
